title: thymus pulegioides subsp. effusus
intro: Thymus pulegioides subsp. effusus, also known as creeping thyme or wild thyme, is a low-growing, spreading herb that belongs to the mint family and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as skin conditions like wounds and ulcers. The health benefits of Thymus pulegioides subsp. effusus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, essential oils, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, promote wound healing, and support overall well-being.
